evals.prompt: I want to add this recipe to my Kroger cart: https://www.bbcgoodfood.com/recipes/classic-victoria-sandwich-recipe
evals.expected_output: Should check Kroger auth status first via GET /v1/kroger/auth/status. If not connected, should call connect_kroger() to get a login URL, ask the user to open it and sign in to Kroger, then call finish_kroger_connection() which polls and saves the session automatically. Should not attempt ingredient matching until auth is confirmed. The user never copies, pastes, or reads back any key.
assertions: Checks Kroger auth status before anything else
If not connected, calls connect_kroger() and returns a login URL
Asks the user to open the URL and sign in to Kroger
Calls finish_kroger_connection() to complete the flow automatically
Does not ask the user to copy or paste any key or token
Does not attempt to match ingredients before auth is confirmed

evals.prompt: I'm connected to Kroger. Shop the ingredients for a pasta carbonara recipe.
evals.expected_output: Should search the recipe index for carbonara using POST /v1/recipes/search or GET /v1/recipes?q=carbonara. Should present matching recipes with titles and source URLs, then confirm which one to shop before proceeding. After the user picks a recipe, calls POST /v1/kroger/match with the recipe_id. Presents each suggested product with name, brand, size, and price. Notes pantry staples the user may already have. Asks user to confirm all picks, swap alternatives, and set quantities before adding anything to the cart.
assertions: Searches recipe index for carbonara
Presents results and asks user to confirm a recipe before proceeding
Calls POST /v1/kroger/match with recipe_id after recipe is confirmed
Shows each suggested product with name, size, and price
Flags pantry staples (eggs, salt, pepper, etc.) as items user may skip
Asks user to confirm all picks before adding
Does not add items without explicit user confirmation

evals.prompt: The ingredient picks look good — add them to my cart for pickup.
evals.expected_output: Calls POST /v1/kroger/cart/add with confirmed UPCs, quantity 1 per item (or user-set quantity), modality PICKUP, and the recipe_id for attribution. On success, reports added_count and tells the user to open the Kroger app or kroger.com to review and complete checkout. Surfaces the recipe source_url and creator attribution. Never claims the order was placed or checkout was completed.
assertions: Calls POST /v1/kroger/cart/add with confirmed UPCs and quantities
Sets modality to PICKUP
Includes recipe_id for attribution
Reports added_count from the response
Directs user to Kroger app or kroger.com to complete checkout
Surfaces source_url and creator attribution
Never states that the order was placed or payment was taken

evals.prompt: Build my grocery cart for tonight's dinner — something with chicken.
evals.expected_output: Should search the recipe index for chicken recipes, present a short list of results with titles and source URLs, and ask the user to choose one before matching ingredients. Should not jump to matching without a confirmed selection. After the user picks a recipe, follows the standard match → confirm → add flow.
assertions: Searches recipe index for chicken recipes
Presents recipe options with titles and source URLs
Asks user to select a specific recipe before proceeding
Does not match ingredients without a confirmed recipe choice
After selection, follows the match → confirm → add flow
